title: Fix lockfile reproducibility runtime on clean runners

sources.reason: Work Item 7 defines the clean-runner lockfile reproducibility requirement.
Current lockfile target and Python interpreter selection.
Clean Runner workflow that must execute the lockfile target.

riskAssessment.reason: The lockfile gate must fail closed and run consistently on clean hosted runners.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized the full review plan and this Work Item is bounded by the declared scope.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

evidence: Make dry-run and compatibility workflow inspection use the selected interpreter directly.
The module invocation exposes the interpreter's actionable ModuleNotFoundError output and the workflow fails closed.
The target compares regenerated requirements-dev.lock bytes with the committed lockfile.

acceptance: The lockfile reproducibility target invokes the selected project interpreter as `$(PYTHON) -m piptools compile` and does not derive a pip-compile executable path from `$(dir $(abspath $(PYTHON)))`.
The clean-runner compatibility workflow executes the lockfile target and reports missing pip-tools or other dependency failures clearly.
Regression tests prove interpreter selection and target command construction, the lockfile target passes locally, and distribution documentation describes the runtime contract.
guidelines: Keep the Make target portable across clean runners with and without a project `.venv`.
Use the project-selected Python interpreter for Python module execution.
Document the clean-runner dependency prerequisite and fail-closed behavior.

intent.problem: The current lockfile reproducibility target derives a pip-compile executable directory from PYTHON, so a clean runner without `.venv` can invoke an invalid path and the compatibility workflow does not exercise the target.
constraints: Preserve hash-locked requirements verification and byte-for-byte comparison.
Do not add a repository-local virtual environment or commit generated lockfile drift.
intent.rationale: Running pip-tools through the same interpreter selected by Make and invoking the target in the clean-runner workflow makes the release gate executable and reproducible across supported environments.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized execution of the complete review plan, including governance files, CI, branches, PRs, merges, and cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
